title: Sweet and Salty Polish Chicken Burger
description: A kid-friendly Polish-inspired chicken burger with a sweet honeysoy glaze and crunchy dill pickle relish, served on a soft bun for a satisfying, kosherfriendly meal.
instructions: Pat the chicken breasts dry and season them lightly with salt and pepper.
In a bowl, whisk together soy sauce, honey, and Dijon mustard to create the glaze.
Place the seasoned chicken in a shallow dish, pour the glaze over, and let marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over medium‑high heat and add olive oil.
Remove chicken from the marinate, reserving the glaze, and cook for 5–6 minutes per side or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
While the chicken is cooking, spread dill pickle relish and fresh dill evenly on the inside of each bun.
When the chicken is done, spoon a bit of the reserved glaze over the patty, then place it on the prepared bun with lettuce.
Serve immediately, optionally with a side of potato chips or a fresh fruit salad.
